posts.hook: Week 4 done. Every exercise we covered — the science behind each one — and what to build on next.
posts.caption:
Week 4 recap — Eye Exercise Deep Dive:

✅ 20-20-20 rule — ciliary muscle mandatory rest every 20 min
✅ Palming — warmth + darkness + oculocardiac reflex, 60 seconds
✅ Focus shifting — near/mid/far accommodation training, full range
✅ Brock String — binocular vision, vergence control, detects suppression
✅ Clock rotations — all 6 extraocular muscles, obliques included
✅ Figure-8 tracking — smooth pursuit gain and phase lag reduction

The full system:
- 20-20-20 → ongoing (every work session)
- Palming → end of day
- Focus shifting → 3 min, daily
- Brock String → 5 min, 3x/week
- Clock rotations → 2 min, daily
- Figure-8 → 3 min, daily

Total: 10–15 min/day for complete eye fitness.

All free, guided: axeo.vision/exercise

posts.hook: The complete eye exercise routine from Week 4 — all 6 exercises, when to do each, and the science behind why.
posts.caption:
A full eye fitness routine — backed by the science covered this week:

20-20-20 rule (ongoing)
Every 20 min of screen work → look 20 feet outside → 20 seconds + 5 deliberate blinks
Why: ciliary muscle mandatory release. Prevents spasm.

Palming (evening)
60–120 seconds of complete darkness, warmed palms cupped over closed eyes.
Why: photoreceptor rest + cortex activity drop + oculocardiac reflex.

Focus shifting (3 min, daily)
Near (60cm) → mid (3–5m) → far (window) → repeat 10×.
Why: full accommodation range. Prevents range compression from screen-only life.

Brock String (5 min, 3×/week)
String from nose to wall, 3 beads. Focus through each bead in sequence.
Why: vergence control and binocular precision. Tests and treats convergence insufficiency.

Clock rotations (2 min, daily)
3 slow clockwise circles + 3 counter-clockwise. Head still.
Why: all 6 extraocular muscles. The obliques almost never trained otherwise.

Figure-8 tracking (3 min, daily)
Trace horizontal infinity symbol with eyes only. Slower = harder.
Why: smooth pursuit gain and phase lag reduction.

All guided, free: axeo.vision/exercise

#EyeExercise #EyeFitness #EyeHealth #Axeo #AxeoVision #VisionTraining #WeekRecap

posts.hook: Week 4 eye exercise deep dive — 6 exercises, the science behind each, and a complete daily routine.
posts.caption:
Week 4 highlights — Eye Exercise Deep Dive:

→ 20-20-20 rule: ciliary muscle mandatory rest. Every 20 min during screen work. Not at a wall — outside.

→ Palming: warmth + complete darkness. Heat vasodilates ciliary body. Darkness rests photoreceptors. 60 seconds.

→ Focus shifting: near → mid → far, 5 seconds each, 10 rounds. Counters accommodation range compression from screen-dominant days.

→ Brock String: binocular vision test and training tool. Reveals suppression and vergence errors most standard exams miss.

→ Clock rotations: all 6 extraocular muscles including the obliques — almost never engaged during screen work.

→ Figure-8 tracking: smooth pursuit training. The eye movement system used by athletes. Gain, phase lag, catch-up saccades — all trainable.

Full routine: 10–15 min/day. All free at axeo.vision/exercise

Week 5: using vision tests to track whether the exercises are actually working.

posts.hook: Week 4 done — eye exercise deep dive recap. One exercise genuinely surprised me.
posts.caption:
r/EyeCare or r/HealthyHabits

Finished Week 4 of my eye exercise experiment — a different exercise each day, all guided through axeo.vision/exercise.

Retrospective on each:

20-20-20 (Monday): Simple but the detail that changed it for me — look OUTSIDE, not at the wall. The wall provides no depth cue for the ciliary muscle. Huge difference.

Palming (Tuesday): Expected nothing. The warmth + darkness combination genuinely reset something by the 90-second mark. The orbital tension I'd normalized as 'just how evenings feel' dropped noticeably.

Focus shifting (Wednesday): Found the mid-distance step hardest. 3–5m requires finding an actual object in your room, not a flat wall. I use a book on a shelf now.

Brock String (Thursday): This was the most diagnostic. I used a piece of kitchen string and three pieces of tape. Discovered my left eye has slightly reduced suppression resistance when fatigued — the X shifts at around 6pm. Never would have known without the string.

Clock rotations (Friday): The oblique positions (lower-right, upper-left) were genuinely stiff. Felt like stretching a muscle I'd ignored for years. Day 3 of doing this and the stiffness is already less.

Figure-8 tracking (Saturday): The 'slower is harder' insight from the research is real. Going very slowly requires real concentration. The catch-up saccades are obvious when you're watching for them.

Week 5: I'm going to run vision tests at axeo.vision at the start and end of the week to track whether any of this is measurably moving the needle. Will report back.

Anyone here done multiple weeks of consistent eye exercises? Curious about longer-term changes.

All exercises free at axeo.vision/exercise — no signup.
